chapter: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: Define a linear equation in two variables and give two examples.
questions.answer: A linear equation in two variables is an equation of the form ax + by + c = 0, where a and b are not both zero. Examples include x + y = 5 and 2x − 3y = 7.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by a pair of linear equations in two variables.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations in two variables consists of two linear equations involving the same variables, usually x and y, considered simultaneously.

questions.question: Explain the graphical representation of a linear equation.
questions.answer: The graphical representation of a linear equation in two variables is a straight line on the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by the solution of a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: The solution of a pair of linear equations is the ordered pair (x, y) that satisfies both equations simultaneously.

questions.question: Explain the graphical method of solving a pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: In the graphical method, both equations are plotted on the same coordinate plane. The point of intersection of the two lines gives the solution.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by a consistent pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: A consistent pair of linear equations is one that has at least one solution.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by an inconsistent pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: An inconsistent pair of linear equations is one that has no solution.

questions.question: Explain the case when a pair of linear equations has a unique solution.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations has a unique solution when their graphs intersect at exactly one point.

questions.question: Explain the case when a pair of linear equations has no solution.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations has no solution when their graphs are parallel and never intersect.

questions.question: Explain the case when a pair of linear equations has infinitely many solutions.
questions.answer: A pair of linear equations has infinitely many solutions when their graphs coincide completely.

questions.question: Explain why the graph of a linear equation is a straight line.
questions.answer: Because the highest power of the variable in a linear equation is one, resulting in a straight-line graph.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by an ordered pair.
questions.answer: An ordered pair (x, y) represents a point in the coordinate plane and a possible solution of a linear equation.

questions.question: Explain why two equations are needed to find two unknowns.
questions.answer: One equation is not sufficient to determine unique values of two variables, so two equations are required.

questions.question: Explain the elimination method of solving linear equations.
questions.answer: In the elimination method, one variable is eliminated by adding or subtracting the equations, making it easier to solve for the other variable.

questions.question: Explain the substitution method of solving linear equations.
questions.answer: In the substitution method, one variable is expressed in terms of the other and substituted into the second equation.

questions.question: Explain the cross-multiplication method of solving linear equations.
questions.answer: The cross-multiplication method is used when equations are in standard form and gives the solution directly using determinants.

questions.question: Explain why graphical method gives only approximate solutions.
questions.answer: Because the accuracy of the solution depends on the scale and precision of the graph drawn.

questions.question: Explain what is meant by a simultaneous equation.
questions.answer: Simultaneous equations are equations that are solved together to find common solutions.

questions.question: Explain the importance of solving pair of linear equations.
questions.answer: They help solve real-life problems involving two unknown quantities.

questions.question: Explain how linear equations are used in daily life.
questions.answer: Linear equations are used in problems involving cost, distance, speed, and other relationships with constant rates.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of intersecting lines in linear equations.
questions.answer: Intersecting lines meet at one point, indicating a unique solution of the equations.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of parallel lines in linear equations.
questions.answer: Parallel lines never intersect, indicating that the equations have no solution.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of coincident lines in linear equations.
questions.answer: Coincident lines lie exactly on each other, indicating infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Explain why algebraic methods are preferred over graphical method.
questions.answer: Because algebraic methods give exact solutions, while graphical method gives approximate solutions.

questions.question: Explain the role of slope in determining the nature of solutions.
questions.answer: Slope determines whether lines intersect, are parallel, or coincide, thus deciding the number of solutions.
